allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking sharpens mental-models-of-reality by forcing a worldview to be rebuilt from facts rather than inherited abstraction.
allies.source_quote: stripping away conventional assumptions to build solutions from fundamental facts

allies.rationale_text: Metacognitive-questioning strengthens mental-models-of-reality by making current maps inspectable before they are mistaken for the territory.
allies.source_quote: critical thinking involves questioning mental models themselves

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias protects an existing map of reality even when disconfirming evidence should update it.
antagonists.source_quote: seek, interpret, and favor information that confirms pre-existing beliefs

structured_tensions.tension_text: Mental-models-of-reality conflicts with confirmation-bias when entrenched worldview filters out disconfirming evidence.
structured_tensions.source_quote: mental-models-of-reality vs confirmation-bias: entrenched worldview filters out disconfirming evidence.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Mental-models-of-reality conflicts with first-principles-thinking when inherited abstractions block decomposition to fundamentals.
structured_tensions.source_quote: mental-models-of-reality vs first-principles-thinking: inherited abstractions block decomposition to fundamentals.

curation_notes.summary: Relation surface stays inside the current graph vocabulary and keeps omission stronger than weak forcing.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 3 target ids or import donor-shaped relation neighborhoods to rescue weak local grounding.
open_questions: Some source-real neighbors remain omitted because the current graph vocabulary would force weaker mappings than this conservative pass allows.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only and is noted only in batch artifacts.
antagonists: No multihop or higher-order semantics were introduced in these Wave 3 files.
structured_tensions: Explicit tensions were preserved only where the raw source clearly earned a current-vocabulary target.
